items.title: Bitcoin’s 20% June crash looks even deadlier on the charts. Here’s why
items.description: Bitcoin dropped by 20% in June, but the monthly chart reveals something far more concerning.

items.title: Live markets: U.S. spot bitcoin ETFs had their worst month ever in June, shedding $4.5 billion
items.description: The record outflow beat the previous worst month by 29% and came on nine consecutive days of redemptions to close the period.

items.title: Anthropic restores AI models Fable, Mythos after the U.S. lifts export controls
items.description: The government cleared Fable 5 and Mythos 5 on June 30, weeks after a cybersecurity finding triggered an export order that froze access for everyone.

items.title: XRP holds above $1 after leverage flush as network activity improves
items.description: Open interest has collapsed from last year’s highs while active addresses and ETF inflows rise, but XRP still needs to reclaim $1.10 before the chart turns cleaner.

items.title: Taiwan’s sweeping crypto law raises the bar with licensing, reserve mandates, and tough penalties
items.description: Taiwan has passed a sweeping new law to regulate its crypto sector, sending the bill to the President for final approval.

items.title: U.S. senators seek to block foreign adversaries from AI technology in new bill
items.description: Senators Tim Scott and Bill Hagerty, who managed to push the crypto GENIUS Act into law, introduced the bill to give the government powers to defend U.S. AI.

items.title: Trump pocketed more than $1 billion from crypto ties as industry headed toward slump
items.description: The president has capitalized massively on crypto, according to his 2025 financial disclosure, making it one of his most lucrative business moves.

items.title: Phantom doubles down on perpetual futures with hire of Hyperliquid market builders
items.description: The crypto wallet is doubling down on perpetual futures after hiring the builders behind one of Hyperliquid's highest-profile market experiments.

items.title: Former Goliath Ventures CEO pleads guilty in $400M crypto Ponzi case
items.description: Former Goliath Ventures CEO Christopher Delgado pleaded guilty to fraud and money laundering and agreed to forfeit properties, vehicles, luxury goods and crypto wallets.

items.title: Taiwan’s legislature passes crypto, stablecoin regulations
items.description: Taiwan’s legislative branch has passed the nation’s first crypto and stablecoin rules, which aim to integrate the country with the global crypto market.

items.title: Anthropic to bring back Fable 5 as US lifts export controls
items.description: Claude Fable 5 will be redeployed with a new set of classifiers designed to identify and block a broader range of cybersecurity-related tasks.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Trump earned more from crypto than real estate in 2025, filings show
items.description: Memecoin sales and royalties made the most money for Trump last year, more than all of his golf courses combined.

items.title: Director of ‘47 Ronin’ who gambled Netflix funds on crypto gets 30 months
items.description: Carl Rinsch will serve two and a half years in prison for taking money meant for a Netflix show to buy Dogecoin, stocks and luxury goods.

items.title: UK crypto investors sue Binance, Changpeng Zhao for $200M
items.description: One Binance customer said he lost the equivalent of more than $132,000 from the crypto exchange’s derivatives offerings before restrictions were imposed on Binance.

items.title: Has Strategy’s capital overhaul put an end to ‘death spiral’ fears?
items.description: Strategy's latest plan includes MSTR and STRC buybacks, expanded cash reserves and even potential Bitcoin sales. Will it be enough to defuse the overheated “death spiral” fears?

items.title: Oil prices wobble as breakdown in U.S.-Iran talks casts doubt over peace process
items.description: Oil prices were lower on Wednesday after Iran said it would not meet with U.S. delegates for talks in Qatar, amplifying concern about the peace process.

items.title: Trump's annual financial disclosure shows more than $580M in crypto-related income
items.description: Trump's 927-page financial disclosure report for 2025 totals more than 900 pages detailing holdings in cryptocurrency and stocks.

items.title: Anthropic says Trump admin has lifted export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5
items.description: The U.S. Department of Commerce lifted the export controls on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models

items.title: Jim Cramer says the AI trade has shifted — and these stocks are leading now
items.description: CNBC's Jim Cramer said Wall Street is rewarding the companies supplying the artificial intelligence boom rather than the technology giants funding it.

items.title: Nike results top estimates even as China sales drop 12%; retailer expects $986 million tariff refund
items.description: Nike was expected to report another quarter of declining sales as the sneaker giant struggles to regain strength under its turnaround strategy.

items.title: Employers who laid off workers citing AI are already starting to regret it
items.description: Companies are realizing artificial intelligence can't do everything after all, prompting them to rehire employees to grow their businesses

items.title: Gold prices fall further after worst quarter in 13 years as interest rate fears hit bullion
items.description: Gold fell further on Wednesday, with bullion continuing its slide after suffering its worst quarter since 2013.

items.title: Japan spent $74 billion propping up the yen. Investors say the real battle is with the Fed
items.description: Yen intervention alone is unlikely to reverse losses while wide U.S.-Japan rate differentials keep supporting the dollar, said analysts.

items.title: Utorg Obtains MiCA License as July 1 Deadline Forces Much of the Industry Out of Europe

items.title: Five things we learnt from Trump’s financial disclosure
items.description: The US president earned more than $1bn from digital currency interests as well as real estate and stock trades

items.title: Uniqlo’s plan to dominate global fashion
items.description: The clothing retailer forged in Japan’s post-bubble economy is pinning its hopes on Europe and North America

items.title: ‘Unprecedented’ plane crash tests Beijing’s sense of security
items.description: Fatal flight of a light aircraft into Chinese capital’s tallest building raises questions about its defences

items.title: The fragility of the world’s economic resilience
items.description: Are we invulnerable or just plain lucky?

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Wf5bKnlOpd0! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

Ways to work around this are explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).


If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: ‘We were stunned’: My daughter, 39, said her mother-in-law gives her more money than we do. Do I call her out?
items.description: “She thanked us for the $100 birthday gift and then announced that her mother-in-law had sent her $400.”

items.title: Netflix’s ‘Owning Manhattan’ star Ryan Serhant expands real estate empire to Texas in ‘strategic’ move
items.description: Reality TV star Ryan Serhant is expanding his fast-growing real estate empire to the Lone Star State.

items.title: ‘Ransom Canyon’ star Josh Duhamel lists L.A. home for $3 million and commits more time to Minnesota cabin
items.description: Hollywood star Josh Duhamel may be parting ways with his life in Los Angeles and dedicating himself to living full-time at his off-grid Minnesota cabin after listing his longtime L.A. home for $2.99 million.

items.title: Your aging parent has a new romance. Here’s how to protect your inheritance — without looking greedy.
items.description: Before your single mom or dad remarries, talk with them about estate-planning issues.

items.title: This threat to the chip rally has surged to its highest level since 2015
items.description: Volatility threatens top gainers including AMD and Micron.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs were supposed to make selloffs less painful. That theory is being put to the test.
items.description: Crypto investors had hoped that increased institutional adoption and a crypto-friendly administration could help the largest cryptocurrency avoid the painful cycles it once experienced.

items.title: Nike earnings crushed Wall Street’s estimates — but here’s the catch
items.description: Nike’s profit and gross margins were buoyed by a tariff refund.

items.title: ‘She is retired’: Do I dip into my 401(k) to pay my mother’s $30,000 credit-card debt?
items.description: “I want her to live on her Social Security instead of using it to pay off her credit-card debt.”

Ways to work around this are explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).


If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: UK house price inflation rises as falling energy costs soften interest rate expectations
items.description: Annual rate of inflation rose to 2.2% in June, up from 1.7% in May, according to Nationwide

items.title: The job market is reaccelerating. Is the economy?
items.description: Plus the bitcoin clown car company crash

items.title: Japan’s ‘Mr Yen’ keeps market guessing on currency intervention
items.description: Traders believe Japanese authorities have changed tactics after yen hit 40-year low against dollar

items.title: European armies already filling holes made by US withdrawals, says Rutte
items.description: Also in this newsletter: Ireland sets out its priorities as it assumes the EU presidency

items.title: India expels thousands of migrants in dead of night
items.description: Deportations exacerbate tense relations with Bangladesh

items.title: Democratic Socialist wins Colorado primary
items.description: Melat Kiros’s victory is latest challenge to Democratic Party leaders ahead of crucial US midterm elections

items.title: FirstFT: Europe defence drive benefits US jobs, Rutte says
items.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Made-in-Germany US weapons and French-US logistics deal

items.title: Introducing: The Story of Money
items.description: A new video podcast from The FT exploring the history of finance

items.title: What to Know About the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement Talks
items.description: July 1 could be the beginning of the end for a trade pact President Trump has long criticized — or the start of a path to its renewal.

items.title: The South Korean Mine at the Center of America’s Tungsten Push
items.description: Deep beneath a South Korean mountain, workers are reviving a mine that could challenge China’s grip on a metal vital to weapons, chips and industry.

items.title: Judges Strike Down Trump Rule on Loan Forgiveness for Public Servants
items.description: Two federal courts have blocked a new Trump administration rule that could have narrowed eligibility for a student loan forgiveness program for public servants.

items.title: During Traffic Stop, N.F.L. Reporter Appeared to Show Off Her Access
items.description: A body-worn camera recording shows Dianna Russini, formerly an “insider” for The Athletic, talking football with an officer and avoiding a citation.

items.title: Trump Pulled in at Least $2.2 Billion in 2025, Financial Disclosure Shows
items.description: The release of a mandatory financial disclosure for 2025 shows that the Trump family’s holdings, particularly the president’s crypto businesses, were stunningly lucrative.

items.title: Neon Buys ‘Artificial,’ a Film About OpenAI, After Amazon Dropped It
items.description: Neon purchased “Artificial,” which focuses on OpenAI’s chief, Sam Altman, after Amazon walked away from it following an investment in the start-up.

items.title: Court Halts Pentagon Rule Requiring Escorts for Journalists
items.description: A federal judge temporarily blocked a new rule that restricted reporters’ access to the Pentagon, in a lawsuit brought by The New York Times.

items.title: Comcast, NBCUniversal Split Drives Deal-Making Speculation
items.description: Almost as soon as the two companies announced a split, analysts rushed to consider potential merger and acquisition targets for each.
